input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyApp.Pages"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent Title="Dashboard"
                      ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent Title="Active"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent Title="Archived"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — standalone flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent Title="Settings"
                      ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
        //       $"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(ProductDetailsPage), typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating from a list item** (e.g. in `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
async void OnProductTapped(object sender, TappedEventArgs e)
{
    if (e.Parameter is int productId)
        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={productId}");
}
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public int ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

### Key points

| Concept | What it does |
|---|---|
| `FlyoutItem` | Top-level entry in the hamburger menu |
| `Tab` inside a `FlyoutItem` | Creates bottom tabs under that flyout item |
| `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` | Lazy-loads each page on first visit |
| `Routing.RegisterRoute` | Enables URI-based navigation to pages not in the visual hierarchy |
| `[QueryProperty]` | Maps query-string params to properties automatically |

The `Products` flyout entry will show a bottom tab bar with **Active** and **Archived**. Dashboard and Settings each appear as simple flyout items with no tabs.
